page: Microsoft Azure D16as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The system includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 10% of evaluated systems. Single-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing also demonstrate strong performance, ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, falls within the average range. A notable performance bottleneck is the large-block read-only memory bandwidth, which is classified as weak (bottom 25%), whereas small-block cached read bandwidth remains average.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the D16as_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-capacity local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for applications such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale text or image processing. However, the weak large-block memory bandwidth and poor performance in large-model LLM prompt processing make it less suitable for memory-intensive data science or large-scale AI inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D16as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Architecturally, it supports AVX-512 and cryptographic accelerations. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU capabilities and strong single-threaded decompression and text processing. However, multi-core operations and memory write speeds are average, while large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is weak. This instance offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads, making it highly suitable for web serving, database operations, and software compilation, while remaining less optimal for memory-bound or large-scale AI inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor and 64 GB of RAM. It offers top-tier single-core performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing power and strong single-core performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong text processing
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
